meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M159/M178 GT Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz • M159/M178 GT • Petrol • V8 6.2L NA / 4.0L TT • 2010–present
hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M159/M178 GT Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M159/M178 are AMG's ultimate V8s — hand-built, sonorous, and immensely powerful, but expensive to maintain and repair. Backed by UK specialist insight [MB-INTERVIEW].

rows.value: H2: M159/M178 GT — Our Verdict & Rating
Confidence: 85% confidence — based on specialist feedback and enthusiast data; these are lower-volume, high-performance engines.

rows.metric: Some manageable repairs (oil leaks), but many failures are all-or-nothing events

rows.value: Available but with wait times; AMG-specific parts can be dealer-only or import-dependent

rows.metric: Reconditioned cost is under 30% of high-value host vehicles (£40k+)

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The M159/M178 are sensational, sonorous V8s — but they're AMG exotica: expensive to maintain and repair — buy with a warranty and a healthy budget for running costs.
rows.value: Best for: Enthusiasts wanting a flagship AMG V8 experience.

rows.metric: Avoid if: You're on a tight budget for maintenance and repairs.

The M159 and M178 represent the pinnacle of Mercedes-AMG's V8 engineering, designed specifically for the brand's halo sports cars: the SLS AMG and the AMG GT. They are hand-built, highly strung, and offer some of the most thrilling driving experiences available.
* Derived from the M156 (E63, C63, S63) but heavily reworked for the SLS AMG
rows.value: * Unique intake, exhaust, camshafts, and engine management

rows.metric: * 571–631 hp, depending on SLS or SLS Black Series specification
* Renowned for its thunderous naturally aspirated soundtrack
* Introduced with the AMG GT in 2014 as a direct successor to the M159
rows.value: * "Hot inside V" turbocharger layout for reduced lag

rows.metric: * 469–730 hp, from GT to GT Black Series
* Shares architecture with the M177 (E63, C63, S63) but with specific AMG GT tuning
* Stunning performance — both engines deliver exceptional power and response
rows.value: * Exquisite sound — M159 is arguably one of the best-sounding V8s ever

rows.metric: * Hand-built quality — "One Man, One Engine" AMG tradition
rows.value: * Robust core — internal components are proven and durable when maintained
* M159 oil consumption — some examples consume oil, especially on track use

rows.metric: * M159/M178 oil leaks — valve cover gaskets, cam cover seals, rear main seal
rows.value: * M178 turbocharger issues — failures reported, especially on tuned or tracked cars

rows.metric: * M178 coolant leaks — water pump, thermostat housing, and related plumbing
rows.value: * M178 high-pressure fuel pump — can fail

rows.metric: * M178 timing chain tensioner — wear reported on higher-mileage examples
rows.value: * Both — engine mounts can fail; low-profile undertray reduces cooling on track
* M159: SLS AMG (C197/R197), SLS AMG GT, SLS AMG Black Series, SLS AMG Electric Drive (not engine)

rows.metric: * M178: AMG GT (C190/R190), AMG GT S, AMG GT C, AMG GT R, AMG GT Black Series, AMG GT 63 4-Door (M177 derivative), Mercedes-AMG One (modified)
rows.value: The M159 and M178 are not engines to be owned on a tight budget, but they reward their keepers with a driving experience few other cars can match. Routine maintenance and proactive repairs are essential, and a warranty is highly recommended.

rows.variantBadge: GT, GT S, GT C, GT R
GT Black Series, GT Track Series

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M159/M178 typically takes 16–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

cta.label: → Compare M159/M178 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an M159/M178 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M159/M178 typically costs £10,000–£18,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by variant and whether ancillaries are included. Fitting adds £1,100–£2,300 depending on labour rates, with specialist AMG workshops essential.

items.question: Is the M159/M178 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M159/M178 rate 4.5★ out of 5 based on specialist feedback [MB-INTERVIEW]. They are robust at their core, but their high performance and exotic nature mean maintenance costs are substantial.

items.question: What is the most common M159/M178 failure?
items.answer: Oil leaks (valve cover gaskets and cam covers) are most common. M178 turbocharger failures and coolant leaks are also reported, especially on tracked or tuned examples.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the M159/M178?
items.answer: The M159 powers the SLS AMG, and the M178 powers the AMG GT (Coupe, Roadster, Black Series) and the AMG GT 4-Door (M177 variant) — flagship sports cars and supercars.

items.question: How long does the M159/M178 last?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance and limited track abuse, the M159/M178 can exceed 100,000 miles. However, many are low-mileage garage queens — and high-mileage examples are rare and require specialist inspection.
